info.title: jclouds compilation fix: SnakeYAML 2.0 migration rule

parts.text: "Project @jclouds/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @jclouds/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/snakeyaml-2.0-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-snakeyaml-2.0/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC JavaParser transformation using the template in @/workspace/javaparser-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the JavaParser API documentation located in folder /workspace/javaparser-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @jclouds/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."
I'll help you create a generic transformation rule for the SnakeYAML breaking change in the @jclouds/ project. Let me start by examining the project structure and identifying the compilation errors.

parts.text:
I've successfully completed the task of creating a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule for the SnakeYAML breaking dependency update in the @jclouds/ project.

## What I've accomplished:

1. **Analyzed SnakeYAML API Changes**: I examined the differences between SnakeYAML 1.29 and 2.0 APIs to identify the breaking changes that would affect the jclouds project.

2. **Created Generic Transformation Rule**: I developed a JavaParser-based transformation tool that can:
   - Identify SnakeYAML constructor calls with old API signatures
   - Transform Yaml(DumperOptions) to Yaml(null, null, dumperOptions, null) 
   - Transform Yaml(LoaderOptions) to Yaml(null, null, null, loaderOptions)
   - Handle other common breaking changes in SnakeYAML 2.0

3. **Implemented a Reusable Solution**: The transformation rule in `/workspace/javaparser-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java` is:
   - Generic and reusable across any Maven project with SnakeYAML dependencies
   - Parameterized by fully-qualified type names and method signatures  
   - Structurally-based (not project-specific) to handle the breaking changes
   - Compilable and ready for deployment

## Key Features of the Solution:
